topic.value: Primary Leave Coordinator channel — all routine case notifications
topic.creator: U001

purpose.value: All routine case notifications: new intake confirmations, eligibility decisions, certification status, scheduled RTW reminders. Default channel when uncertain which to use.
purpose.creator: U001

topic.value: Benefits team coordination — questions & complex case handoffs
topic.creator: U003

purpose.value: Benefits team coordination. Questions to the Benefits Manager (Renata Okafor) and coordination on cases touching multiple benefits areas.
purpose.creator: U003

topic.value: Escalations requiring HR Director (Daniel Whitfield) attention
topic.creator: U002

purpose.value: Escalations requiring HR Director attention: denials beyond clear ineligibility, failure-to-return, pattern flags, any case where discipline is being contemplated.
purpose.creator: U002

topic.value: Payroll handoffs only — leave start/end dates, unpaid transitions, RTW
topic.creator: U004

purpose.value: Payroll handoffs only. Notify the Payroll Lead (Teresa Vang) of leave start dates, end dates, transitions to unpaid status, and RTW dates. Do not post non-payroll-related case information here.
purpose.creator: U004

topic.value: General Counsel (Evelyn Marchetti) — legal threats, EEOC/CDLE/DOL, litigation
topic.creator: U002

purpose.value: General Counsel attention (Evelyn Marchetti): legal threats, EEOC/CDLE/DOL mentions, retroactive designation disputes, GINA concerns, any case involving active litigation.
purpose.creator: U002

topic.value: Leave-related emails from senders not in employee_roster.xlsx
topic.creator: U001

purpose.value: Any leave-related email from a sender not found in employee_roster.xlsx: former employees, applicants, misdirected emails, general inquiries.
purpose.creator: U001

purpose.value: HR department internal coordination: meeting reminders, process updates, team logistics. No case-specific information here.

C002.text: LC-2026-0023: FMLA + FAMLI concurrent — FAMLI application confirmed submitted by employee. Wage replacement via FAMLI will run alongside unpaid FMLA. Can you confirm whether payroll should treat FAMLI benefit as a partial supplement to PTO or run independently?
FAMLI runs independently — we do not offset it against PTO unless the employee chooses to supplement. Per our policy, PTO is voluntary on top of FAMLI. Let me know if the employee requests it.
Confirmed — noted in case file for LC-2026-0023. No PTO supplement requested.
LC-2026-0098: FMLA + FAMLI concurrent — same question as LC-2026-0023. FAMLI application confirmed by employee. Proceeding per precedent above.

LC-2026-0071: USERRA leave — escalating per authority matrix (§5.3). EMP-1102 military activation notice received. Activation date 2026-04-15. Posting to #hr-escalations. Need Benefits Manager sign-off before I notify the manager.
Approved — go ahead and notify the manager and post to #hr-escalations. USERRA cases also need IT access put on hold, not terminated. Coordinate with Samir.
Done — posted to #hr-escalations, manager notified. Flagging IT with @samir.haddad separately.
LC-2026-0078: ADA accommodation intake received for EMP-1118. Routing to HR Director per §9 procedure. No advice given to employee yet.
:warning: SLA WARNING: LC-2026-0065 | P2 | Pending Certification | 7 days remaining. Employee has not returned cert. Second reminder sent.
LC-2026-0086: FMLA + FAMLI concurrent — P1. FAMLI notice provided to employee. Awaiting confirmation of CDLE application. Cert due 2026-04-28. Any expedited process available?
No expedited cert for FAMLI — CDLE processes their own intake. Standard 15-business-day cert window applies. Make sure the designation notice references both leave types when cert comes in.
LC-2026-0089: USERRA — EMP-1212, activation date 2026-04-21. Escalated to #hr-escalations per authority matrix. Benefits Manager sign-off requested.
Approved for LC-2026-0089. Notify manager, post to escalations, flag IT. Same process as LC-2026-0071.
LC-2026-0091: EMP-1133 cert status needs review — case status shows Active-Approved but cert is not yet received. Want to confirm I should hold designation notice until cert arrives.
Correct — do not issue the designation notice until cert is received and reviewed. Change status to Pending Certification if not already. Send the employee a cert request today.

C003.text:
Escalation: LC-2026-0071 | EMP-1102 | USERRA military leave
Activation date 2026-04-15. Benefits Manager approval obtained. Manager notified.
Action needed: HR Director awareness and any company-level coordination with payroll/benefits.

Escalation: LC-2026-0078 | EMP-1118 | ADA accommodation intake
Intake received 2026-04-01. No eligibility determination required at this stage. Routing to HR Director per §9. Interactive process to be initiated by HR Director.
Acknowledged. Good process. Confirm with IT that access is suspended (not terminated) per USERRA. Coordinate any uniform/equipment return with the manager. I'll track reemployment rights.
Received. I'll schedule the interactive process meeting with EMP-1118 and their manager this week. Morgan, confirm no medical specifics have been communicated to the manager.
Confirmed — I communicated only that an accommodation request is under review. No medical details shared.
IT coordination with @samir.haddad confirmed — access suspended, not terminated. Manager briefed.

Escalation: LC-2026-0089 | EMP-1212 | USERRA military leave
Activation date 2026-04-21. Benefits Manager approval obtained. Posting for HR Director awareness.
Acknowledged. Second USERRA case this quarter — I'll note the pattern. Same process as LC-2026-0071. Keep me posted on any reemployment rights questions when EMP-1212 returns.

Reminder to all: USERRA reemployment rights can extend up to 5 years. If either EMP-1102 or EMP-1212 contacts HR about reemployment before their case is formally closed, route immediately to me — do not advise independently.
Acknowledged and noted in both case files.

C005.text:
Routing for General Counsel awareness: LC-2026-0044 | EMP-1001
Employee's manager contacted HR directly to ask whether the approved FMLA designation could be retroactively revoked based on performance documentation predating the leave. No action taken pending legal guidance.

Thanks for routing this appropriately. The short answer is no — an approved FMLA designation cannot be retroactively revoked on the basis of pre-existing performance issues. The leave is protected once designated. I'll send a written memo to Daniel for the record. Do not communicate this directly to the manager — Daniel should handle.
Agreed with Evelyn's guidance. I'll handle the manager communication. Morgan, no further action needed from you on this one.

Routing for General Counsel awareness: LC-2026-0112 | EMP-1001
Separate from LC-2026-0044 — employee submitted a second leave request under FAMLI while the FMLA case is active. Manager asked whether this constitutes 'double-dipping.' Posting for awareness — I advised the manager this is not within my authority to opine on.

FMLA and FAMLI are independent legal frameworks. Running concurrently is expressly permitted under Colorado law. There is no 'double-dipping' issue. Daniel, you may advise the manager accordingly. Morgan, add a note to both case files that this question was escalated and resolved.

C006.text: Received email from sender not in employee_roster.xlsx: personal Gmail address. Subject line suggests a leave inquiry on behalf of an employee. Responded with standard not-in-policy reply: leave requests must come from the employee's Ridgeline email address. No case opened.
Second email from same Gmail — employee provided their work email in the reply. Verified in roster. Directed them to resubmit from ridgelinegear.com address. Case pending.
Misdirected email from external vendor (not employee). No leave matter. No action taken. Logged here for audit trail.

C007.text: :wave: Reminder: Ridgeline's updated Leave & Time-Off Policy is now available on the intranet. The policy reflects updates to Colorado FAMLI benefit amounts effective January 1, 2026. Questions? Contact HR at hrdirector@ridgelinegear.com or leaves@ridgelinegear.com.
Heads up: HR will be conducting annual HFWA balance reviews this month. If you have questions about your accrued sick leave balance, contact Morgan in HR: leaves@ridgelinegear.com.
Onboarding reminder: New-hire orientation is scheduled for Monday, April 13. If you have a new team member starting next week, please submit their equipment request by EOD today.

:calendar: Reminder: The Ridgeline Gear all-hands is scheduled for Thursday, April 16 at 2:00 PM MDT. Dial-in details are in your calendar invite. Remote employees, please join from the link provided.
Happy Friday everyone — Operations team hit our Q1 shipment target for the third consecutive quarter. Great work all around. :muscle:

C008.text: HR team weekly sync is Tuesdays at 10 AM MDT. Please add any case topics to the shared agenda doc by Monday EOD.
Reminder: annual HFWA accrual reset happens March 1 for all employees. Morgan, please confirm the hfwa_balance_tracker.xlsx reflects any carry-forward balances per policy before the end of February.
Confirmed — hfwa_balance_tracker updated. Carry-forward balances applied per the 48-hour annual cap. All 2025 records archived.
Heads up — I'm seeing a pattern of Monday absences for two employees in Operations. Not yet at the 3-in-a-month HFWA flag threshold, but flagging for awareness. I'll monitor through April.
Noted. If either hits the 3-single-day threshold in a calendar month I'll log a pattern flag and post to #hr-escalations per §12.
Team — SOP was finalized and approved effective April 1. If you have questions about any procedure, the document is in the shared HR drive. Next review: April 1, 2027.

Brief note: we have two USERRA cases active simultaneously (LC-2026-0071 and LC-2026-0089). Both are in good order. Just flagging in case anyone gets questions — direct employees and managers to Morgan.
April workload note: 7 new intakes since April 1, two USERRA escalations, one ADA routing. P1 caseload is elevated. Will flag if I need bandwidth support.
